title: Make project context optional, inferred, and autosaved
brief: Rethink the project context UI so setup can continue after sign-in or be skipped, opening Project context from the sidebar exposes the form, Infer from repo fills that form, and context changes save automatically.
description: Simplify project context onboarding and editing. Project context should not block sign-in/setup. Users can add it immediately after sign-in or skip it, reopen it from the sidebar, infer repository details into the editable form, and rely on autosave rather than a manual Save context action.
acceptanceCriteria: Project context is presented as an optional step after agent sign-in and can be skipped.
Opening Project context from the sidebar shows the editable context form.
Infer from repo updates and visibly fills the project context form with inferred values.
Edits to project context are saved automatically with clear saving, saved, and error feedback.
The manual Save context action is removed or no longer required.
Existing project inference and persistence behavior remains functional.
qaChecklist: Verify a first-time user can sign in and skip project context.
Verify Project context can be reopened from the sidebar.
Verify Infer from repo populates visible form fields.
Verify typed edits persist without clicking a save button.
Verify autosave status feedback and error handling.
designQaChecklist: The optional nature of project context is clear without making the feature feel unimportant.
Primary and secondary actions have a clear hierarchy.
The form remains usable in the narrow VS Code sidebar.
constraints: Follow existing VS Code webview and storage patterns.
Do not include unrelated uncommitted changes from the main checkout.
agentNotes: Implemented in an isolated task worktree because the main checkout has unrelated uncommitted changes. After CLI sign-in the optional Project Context drawer opens and can be skipped. Context fields debounce-save after 700ms and flush on close, with race protection for edits made during an in-flight save. Repo inference now expands the structured form, fills validation commands, detected inference data, and empty architecture notes while preserving user-written architecture notes.

qaNotes.message: Verified optional post-sign-in context, inference population, autosave race handling, and all automated validation commands.
